items.title: TOP-PRIORITY owner request: yardage-book tee-shot overlays on the in-round satellite hole map (blue/white/red 200/150/100 plates from green center + fairway-bunker front/back carries), tee-shot context only
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Previously: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #137) @8de8c0d (cycle 113, 2026-07-12), NOTICEABLE (on-course satellite-map feature). Fable plan specs/tee-shot-yardage-overlays-plan.md. Frontend-only pure module frontend/src/lib/map/tee-shot-overlays.ts (+ tee-shot-overlays.test.ts, 25 pinned vitest) consumed by GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx via a new optional mappedHoles prop (inert when absent -> CourseSearch/CourseScoutMap//map/course unaffected); wired through InlineHoleDiagram (holeIndex) + RoundPageClient (mappedHolesIndex). Data reality: the honest geometry (real bunker Polygons + the dogleg-aware golf=hole centerline LineString) lives in CourseData.holes[i].features (already client-side for the paper HoleDiagram); the map's CourseCoordinates.hazards are centroids only, so this reads features directly. PLATES: walk the golf=hole centerline green-first (60y green-end sanity guard) in UNROUNDED float meters, green-CENTER offset so a '150' is truly 150 to center, omit targets beyond path length; centerline REQUIRED (no chord fallback -> dogleg honesty); mechanical honesty guard greenEndLateralOffsetMeters omits ALL plates when the way's green-end is >7y perpendicular off the line to green center (catches offset-way mislabel; leaves legitimate downpath doglegs intact). BUNKERS: Polygon outer-ring vertices only, carry projected in a tee-origin equirectangular frame (matches backend hazards._xy_m / _project_onto_polyline), front=min/back=max round-to-5, fairway predicate on RAW carry (in [100,330]) AND >=45y radial from green center (excludes greenside) AND min|lateral|<=45y (corridor); centroid-only bunkers SKIPPED (never a fabricated range); display cap 2 inline / 4 fullscreen (smallest-lateral selection). VISIBILITY: pure stateless predicate (position null OR !gpsOnHole OR dist(pos,tee)<=40y), native overlays touched only on the boolean FLIP (no GPS-jitter churn). Par-3 fully suppressed. RENDER (iOS iconUrl can't do data-URL icons — verified Map.swift): plates = native addCircles (4m; colors reuse the existing tee palette #2e5aa8/#f2efe6/#b23a2e), bunker near-edge dot = native circle (3m), carry TEXT = DOM paper chips styled like the tap-target pill (right edge, AnimatePresence). All native calls gated on mapReadyRef; overlays id-tracked in a separate teeShotCircleIdsRef + routed through the coalescing camera queue (no leak across hole swipes). reviewer (FABLE, trust-critical geometry) SHIP: hand-verified green-center offset, dogleg plate on 2nd leg, carry orientation (a real TDD-caught inversion fix — tee-first reversal + teeAlongM subtract), unrounded walk, honesty (synthetic hd.front/back never read, degenerate->EMPTY), onMapReady gating, no regression, security nil; 4 non-blocking, #1(plate self-check)/#3(raw-carry predicate)/#4(test6 deadband) folded in, #2(pre-existing mount/queue interleave) out of scope. qa PASS + CI Frontend+Backend SUCCESS on head 3ca8324 (lint/tsc/vitest 2416/build 19-page/voice 278). designer BLOCKING->APPROVE on rendered pixels: 1st pass BLOCKED (inline chip stack overlapped RoundPageClient's Par/Yardage/Hcp stats pill; cap-4 too dense inline) -> fixed (chip top 12->54 = 11px gap measured at 390x844+375x667; inline cap 2) -> APPROVE (both blockers resolved, no regression). OWNER REAL-DEVICE EYEBALL NEEDED (not a gate fail): on-satellite plate/dot contrast + size across live pinch-zoom (sandbox has no live Maps tiles — Maps-key credential policy), and the AnimatePresence fade as GPS crosses the 40y tee-zone boundary on a real walk.
items.why: Owner-requested directly with a reference PDF of a real Jones yardage book: 'add some details like this for TEE SHOTS ONLY... the blue/white/red indicators on the map to show 200,150,100 yards out... showing the yardages to the front and back of the bunkers (if there are any fairway bunkers) from the tee box are super helpful... only when we are on the tee box / open a new hole... makes it look more like a true yardage book.' Explicitly rejected the per-dot fairway-yardage scatter as 'too much.' This is the crux yardage-book surface — trust-critical (the numbers a golfer reads MUST be right) and honest (no geometry -> silent absence, never invented).

items.title: Owner bug (v1.1.5): Bethpage Red hole 1 shows no tee-shot overlays and no hole centerline — backfill Red course geometry into the prod DB
items.resolution: AUDIT DONE cycle 115 (2026-07-12); FIX is an owner-gated prod write. Method: OSM-source audit (local Overpass, no DB/secrets) via new backend/scripts/audit_course_coverage.py (landed on integration/next @9c95dad, bundle PR #139, SILENT dev tooling; generalises the Black-only diag_bethpage.py to any --target and adds centerline-presence + overlay-ready verdict per hole). Finding: OSM has FULL Bethpage Red geometry — 18/18 holes overlay-ready (Red H1 466y par4: centerline + green + 2 fairway + 3 tees; 0 bunkers is an honest OSM gap). So upstream OSM is NOT the gap; root cause (high confidence) is Red not (fully) ingested into the prod DB (only Black is; ingest defaults to Black). Verified fix end-to-end via dry-run (no prod write): uv run backend/scripts/ingest_osm_course.py --target-course Red --course-key osm-bethpage-red --course-name 'Bethpage Red' — assembles 18 holes / 231 features under a NEW osm-bethpage-red course UUID 269e1f2e-65cc-5cf6-a9b0-f5908e298155, SEPARATE from Black (cannot corrupt Black; reviewer verified all 5 data-safety claims TRUE @9c95dad). GATED: prod DB write — owner (or an approved session) runs it against prod, then verifies with audit_course_coverage.py --target Red. SECONDARY: the owner's EXISTING Red round only gains overlays if its mappedCourseId points at the new Red row; new rounds via course search will. INFRA BLOCKER for the DB-side audit: the looper/prod Secrets Manager DATABASE_URL has a stale password (blocked the sanctioned SSM read-probe); refresh it to re-enable DB coverage checks.
items.why: Owner reported on TestFlight v1.1.5 (Bethpage Red hole 1, round in progress): no tee-shot yardage plates AND no hole centerline line. The overlays draw only from real CourseData.holes[i].features geometry with honest absence, so a bare hole means missing DB geometry. Fixing Red and generalising coverage audits protects the yardage-book feel on the owner's home course.

items.title: TOP-PRIORITY owner request: iOS edge-swipe-from-left to go back on ALL pages (except the in-round yardage-book hole page, which keeps prev-hole swipe)
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Previously: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #137) @9873d19 (cycle 111, 2026-07-12), NOTICEABLE (app-wide navigation). Mechanism (fable plan specs/universal-swipe-back-plan.md): a global JS left-screen-edge touch detector — WKWebView allowsBackForwardNavigationGestures rejected (static export output:'export' single-document pushState; native gesture renders stale snapshots for same-document history entries and is webview-global with no per-route exclusion). New frontend/src/components/nav/BackSwipe.tsx ('use client', renders null) mounted once in app/layout.tsx beside FloatingTabBar; document capture-phase passive touch listeners (never preventDefault). Pure core backSwipeGesture.ts: isEdgeStart (start x <= safeAreaLeft+24px), decideBackSwipe (rightward only, |dx|>1.8|dy|, flick <600ms&>=70px OR long-drag >=35%vw), isDisqualified (vertical-scroll early cancel), + guarded memoized readSafeAreaLeft() invalidated on orientationchange/resize. Route predicate shouldEnableBackSwipe.ts (sibling of shouldShowTabBar.ts): /round/new true, startsWith('/round/') false (covers /round/[id] AND /round/view), /map/course false, else true; re-checked at touchend for mid-gesture route races. Back semantics: router.back() gated by a session navigation-depth counter (window.history.length rejected as unusable in single-document export); depth 0 off-home -> router.push('/') so a deep-linked page never dead-ends or pops out of the app; depth 0 at '/' silent no-op; 350ms refire lockout. Conflict avoidance: edge-zone+direction+time gating; SwipeableRow (rightward swipe-to-delete, highest risk) gets an onPointerDownCapture edge-claim (stopPropagation on edge starts, the proven RoundPageClient trick) so a row never drags on an edge start; data-no-backswipe escape-hatch attribute on the three bottom sheets (CaddieSheet/LooperSheet/VoiceRoundSetupRealtime) + SwipeableRow confirm backdrop + FloatingTabBar root. 34 pure unit tests (backSwipeGesture 22 incl. pinned landscape boundary 83 true/84 false + shouldEnableBackSwipe 12). reviewer SHIP (all named failure modes — hijack in-content h-swipe / break hole nav / pop out of app / fire on non-edge / route race / listener leaks — traced and unfalsifiable; security surface nil, client-only touch math; tests genuine). qa PASS (gates on the integrated tree: lint / tsc / vitest 2379 / build 19-page static / voice 278; browser touch-event verification against the REAL listeners confirmed home<->tee-time history.back, deep-link push('/') fallback, home no-op, /round/ + /map/course exclusions, /round/new included, caddie-sheet data-no-backswipe blocks). designer APPROVE (threshold-only + haptic('light') — no follow-the-finger chrome — is the calm/native/yardage-book-correct call; no SaaS drift). Owner real-device pass needed (documented, not a gate fail): native map-pan non-hijack, native touch feel/haptic timing, and the SwipeableRow drag-vs-edge-flick distinction (framer needs real touch->pointer translation a headless browser can't produce).
items.why: Owner-requested directly: 'On every single page you should be able to swipe to go back... if I go to tee time from the home page, swiping back should take me there.' Standard iOS edge-back is an implicit expectation on every screen; its absence made client-side routes feel trapped. The in-round yardage-book page is excluded because its horizontal swipe already means prev/next hole.

items.title: TOP-PRIORITY owner bug: STT keyterm/priming context rendered as a user bubble in the LIVE caddie chat (and 'answered')
items.resolved: 6a68078 (cycle 105, integration/next / PR #136). Root cause: the golf keyterm/priming string (backend/app/caddie/keyterms.py build_transcription_prompt) is injected CORRECTLY as an STT hint at session.audio.input.transcription.prompt (never a conversation item, never in session.instructions) -- the leak is DOWNSTREAM: gpt-4o-transcribe hallucinates its own transcription.prompt back as the input transcript when server VAD false-triggers on silence/ambient noise (verbatim OR paraphrased -- the owner's two variants). frontend/src/lib/voice/realtime.ts then rendered ANY non-empty transcript as a role:'user' bubble; the model, hearing the same noise, independently replied 'Didn't catch that'. Fix (fable plan specs/caddie-context-leak-plan.md): new pure client classifier frontend/src/lib/voice/priming-echo.ts isPrimingEcho(transcript) wired as a drop-guard in the input_audio_transcription.completed handler (before onMessage AND before orderForUserTranscript -- ordering-safe, reservations are identity-keyed per realtime-ordering.ts), with a len-only realtime_priming_echo_dropped telemetry breadcrumb. Classifier drops iff A: signature phrase /player'?s clubs/ or /golf vocabulary/ (present in both owner variants, never in real speech), B: >=10 distinct GOLF_KEYTERMS (multi-word masked so 'double bogey' != 'bogey'; worst plausible real turn ~7), or C: >=3 segments ALL bare hazard nouns. Conservative: false-negatives render (safe), false-positives effectively impossible. Belt-and-suspenders backend rider: order-preserving hazard dedupe within the 'This hole:' sentence in keyterms.py. reviewer SHIP (no security/correctness blocker; /security-review clean -- regex from hardcoded GOLF_KEYTERMS only, escapeRegExp, no ReDoS, telemetry logs len not body, dedupe stays confined to transcription.prompt). qa PASS (7 gates on head a0c52d2/6a68078: lint / tsc / build / voice 278-278 / vitest 30-30 [priming-echo + realtime-warm] / ruff / backend test_transcription_prompt 10-10; classifier tests assert BOTH owner strings dropped AND real turns kept incl. adversarial dense-keyterm turn). designer APPROVE (pure subtraction -- no new chrome/placeholder where the bubble was; bubble styling untouched; legit user/typed turns unaffected).
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-11 (bundle #136 merged @71f8910; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.4 build 202607120035). Previously: LANDED on integration/next @6a68078 (bundle PR #136), NOTICEABLE.
items.why: Owner-reported directly with 2 screenshots (v1.1.3, on-course LIVE caddie): the priming/context string ('Player's clubs: GW, LW... This hole: trees, trees... Golf vocabulary: birdie, bogey...') showed as a dark right-aligned user bubble and the caddie tried to answer it ('Didn't catch that -- say again?'). Core-feature correctness: the priming must bias STT recognition but NEVER enter the visible/processed conversation.

items.title: Follow-up: lone 'Didn't catch that -- say again?' caddie reply fires on VAD false-triggers (noise), now with NO preceding user bubble
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-11 (bundle #136 merged @71f8910; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.4 build 202607120035). Previously: Landed @76d8c95 on integration/next (cycle 107, PR #136, NOTICEABLE caddie correctness): chose option (b) — client-side suppression of the lone no-input clarifier BUBBLE. New pure module noinput-clarifier.ts (isNoInputClarifier/couldBecomeClarifier, closed-vocabulary+marker+digit-ban, mirrors priming-echo.ts) + realtime.ts response<->input correlation + hold/suppress/release state machine (grace-timer bounded, no strand). NO VAD/threshold/mic/noise_reduction change (owner-gated line respected; option (a) untouched). Bias-to-keep: any real/failed/typed input turn never held. reviewer SHIP, qa PASS (lint/tsc clean, vitest 2288/2288, build ok, voice-smoke 278/278), designer APPROVE. Accepted residual: caddie still SPEAKS 'say again?' audio (only the bubble is suppressed; response.cancel deliberately avoided). Non-blocking follow-up filed: caddie-noise-clarifier-followups.
items.why: Flagged by BOTH reviewer and designer during caddie-context-leak review (cycle 105) as an explicitly-scoped-OUT residual of that fix (specs/caddie-context-leak-plan.md §6). The 'Didn't catch that' reply is the Realtime MODEL independently responding to committed noise-audio (input transcription is a side-channel; the model hears audio directly) -- a DIFFERENT system from the transcript-render leak that was fixed. Now that the fake priming user-bubble is correctly dropped, a VAD false-trigger on ambient noise can produce a lone caddie utterance with no user bubble above it -- reads as the caddie talking to itself (arguably MORE visually odd than before). Options (each its own plan): (a) tune server VAD so it doesn't fire on false-triggers -- the OPENAI_REALTIME_VAD=semantic_vad lever + server_vad threshold/noise_reduction knobs exist in realtime_relay.py but are owner-gated / explicitly hands-off in the context-leak fix; (b) suppress rendering (or generation) of a clarification reply that is a direct response to a turn we know was noise (e.g. no non-empty user transcript for that turn). User-facing caddie surface -> designer-gated. Do NOT touch VAD thresholds without an owner decision.

items.title: Follow-up nits from caddie-noise-clarification-reply review (cycle 107) — non-blocking
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Previously: DONE on integration/next (cycle 109, @d187254, PR #137 bundle) as a focused realtime-voice reliability hardening pass. Plan on fable (specs/caddie-voice-reliability-hardening-plan.md). All 3 nits fixed + 2 audit fixes, all sandbox-verified (NO VAD/mic/gain/turn_detection/commit-timing change -- owner-gated line respected; reviewer grep-confirmed zero VAD surface): (1) attribution race -- replaced single-trigger triggerItemByResponse (LIFO pop) with candidate-SET triggerItemsByResponse + classifyCandidates() aggregate (ANY real=>release, ALL noinput=>suppress, else pending); a real utterance's clarifier can never be swallowed (strictly more conservative than shipped). (2) map lifecycle -- idempotent finishResponse() prunes per-turn + caps MAX_INPUT_CLASS_ENTRIES=64 / MAX_TRACKED=128 (evict-oldest, skip live candidates). (3) held-turn copy -- new pure lib/caddie/live-copy.ts liveEmptyStateHint 'speaking' branch so empty-state never says 'is listening' while footer says 'speaking' (designer APPROVE). (4a) response.done GA-shape id fallback (evt.response.id) -- no stuck-partial bubble. (4b) `if(!this.dc)return` post-cleanup guard -- late frames can't re-arm idle timer / leak to rebound handlers. New tests realtime-attribution (A1-A7) + realtime-lifecycle (L1-L3,4a,4b) + live-copy + ordering O1 + CaddieSheet.realtime, all proven RED pre-fix / GREEN post-fix; realtime-noinput.test.ts + priming-echo.test.ts unchanged (regression lock). reviewer SHIP, qa PASS (lint/tsc/vitest 2322/build/voice 278/ruff, backend diff empty), designer APPROVE. Non-blocking follow-up noted by designer: closed/error empty-state fallback still maps to the 'listening' hint (unreachable in live render today -- paused/classic-fallback intercepts; ticket only if that guarantee changes).
items.why: Three non-blocking nits raised by reviewer + designer on the SHIPPED no-input clarifier suppression (@76d8c95), tracked so they aren't lost. (1) Narrow item-attribution race in realtime.ts: pendingSpeechItems.pop() (LIFO+clear) at response.created can misattribute a REAL-but-garbled turn's clarifier to a phantom noise speech_started that fired inside the sub-second turn->response.created window, hiding a LEGIT clarifier bubble (audio still plays; no substantive answer ever lost -- degrades to the accepted audio-only residual). Fix idea: on a 'real' transcript, re-scan triggerItemByResponse/heldResponses and un-suppress. (2) triggerItemByResponse + inputClassByItem maps are pruned only at cleanup() -> one entry/turn growth within a session (bounded by 90s idle-disconnect; not a real leak). Fix idea: delete the pair's entries when a response finalizes/resolves. (3) Cosmetic: during a held/suppressed turn CaddieSheet.tsx empty-state copy ('...is listening') can briefly (<=2s grace) disagree with the footer status ('speaking'). Fix idea: branch the empty-state copy on 'speaking' too. All low-severity; batch into a future voice-caddie pass, not worth its own cycle.

items.title: Follow-up: complete the deferred money-settling game formats (wolf true-transfer, opponent/team pickers)
items.why: Deferred from cycle 100 (tournament-settlement-honesty, @2e17261 on integration/next / PR #135). That cycle made the picker HONEST by only offering a stake for formats that truly settle zero-sum (STAKE_GAME_IDS = {skins, match, nassau}) and PREVENTING silently-truncated games. Three capabilities were intentionally deferred as product/design-shaped, each a real feature the owner may want later: (1) WOLF as a money game -- computeWolf (games.ts ~836-862) is NOT zero-sum today: lone-mode credits the wolf +/-3 and debits no one; partner-mode awards +1/+1 to the winning pair and debits no one (reviewer falsified: a lone-wolf win netted +$6, sum != 0). To re-enable wolf stakes, make the deltas TRUE transfers (lone win: wolf +3, each other -1; lone loss: wolf -3, each other +1; partner: distribute the pot across the losing side -- a real rules decision) then re-add 'wolf' to SETTLEABLE_FORMATS + STAKE_GAME_IDS and add a decided-multi-hole zero-sum test (the new SETTLEABLE_FORMATS property test in settlement.test.ts will enforce it). (2) MATCH-PLAY opponent picker -- match play is restricted to exactly-2 rosters (prevents silent drop of players 3+); a >2 roster needs an opponent-selection UI writing game.settings.matchPlayPlayers (engine already supports it, games.ts:740-741). (3) TEAM-ASSIGNMENT UI -- unlocks stakes for the team formats currently non-settling-by-construction in the picker (vegas is team-only/unconstructible today; bestBall/scramble/threePoint need teams). Each is its own plan+tests; keep [[no-fake-data-fallbacks]]: never re-offer a stake until the format settles zero-sum end-to-end.
items.resolution: PART 1 (WOLF) SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (cycle 110, @741e6ac, bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Also in this bundle: threePoint zero-sum rounding-drift fix (cycle 112, @1ee8427, tournament settlement coverage hardening — real odd-cent-stake money bug). Option A per specs/tournament-wolf-settlement-plan.md (fable): computeWolf now emits a TRUE zero-sum per-hole pointsDelta (lone win wolf +3 / each of 3 opponents -1; lone loss mirror; partner win winners +1 / losers -1; partner loss mirror; ties and any incomplete-data hole -- missing pick, missing wolf score, or fewer than all 3 opponent scores in lone mode -- emit an EMPTY delta / $0), and settlement.ts settles money = totals x pointValue and re-adds 'wolf' to SETTLEABLE_FORMATS (STAKE_GAME_IDS derives it, so buildRoundGames carries wolf's stake again). Zero-sum enforced every hole; wolf registered in the SETTLEABLE_FORMATS property-test fixture (asymmetric p1 +$12 / p2 -$4 / p3 -$8 / p4 $0 at $2) which now passes. Data model already captured the pick (types.ts wolfHoleChoices/wolfOrderPlayerIds + in-round GameResults.tsx UI) -- NO type/backend/migration change. Scoreboard now shows negative point totals for hole losers (T.errorInk, matching Hammer/BBB) across GameResults/GameLeaderboards/LeaderboardSheet. reviewer SHIP (hand-recomputed fixture, verified every branch balances), qa PASS (vitest 2336/build/voice 278/ruff; CI backend SUCCESS), designer BLOCKING->APPROVE after the negative-total color/sign fix. Commits 9ba12db (engine+tests) + 741e6ac (designer fix + reviewer hardening). STILL OPEN (item stays ready): PART 2 match-play >2-player opponent picker and PART 3 team-assignment UI (vegas/bestBall/scramble/threePoint) -- both genuinely design/UX-gated, filed for an owner product decision; do NOT build until the owner decides how the golfer records opponents/teams.

items.title: Fetch real tee-time availability for EVERY course (API → scrape → AI-call ladder)

Ladder = 4 rungs: API → widget-XHR replay → headless render → AI-call → honest-empty. Sequenced S4a-S4f below. Most of the epic is engine-adapter breadth + per-course capability detection, NOT exotic scraping. OWNER DIRECTIVE 2026-07-10 (screenshot, Marine Park 'No online booking — call the pro shop'): showing a bare phone number is NOT good enough. Every course MUST return real availability in SOME form. Ladder: (1) clean booking-engine API where one exists (foreUP live; EVALUATE adding Chronogolf/Lightspeed/GolfNow-EZLinks/TeeItUp APIs), (2) WEB SCRAPE the rendered availability widget where no clean JSON API exists, (3) AI phone-call the pro shop (PR #124 caller) as the final fallback for phone-only courses. PLAN FIRST on Fable (per fable-for-plan-agents): per-engine option eval, scraping feasibility+ToS/legal+infra (headless browser for JS-rendered widgets), caching (GolfAPI-style budget discipline), integration with the reachability router.

items.title: Bethpage 7/11 guide-validation conflict: DIAGNOSED — carry-tolerance vs bunker footprint (decision needed)
items.why: INVESTIGATION 2026-07-09 (cycle 39, OFFLINE from committed fixture tests/fixtures/bethpage_overpass.json — same data prod ingested; no prod SSM needed). Holes 7 & 11 were persistently honest-empty (research ~4x each fails validate_guide). ROOT CAUSE = (c) validator over-strict, NOT (a) geometry or (b) OSM. EVIDENCE: (a) ruled out — extract_hole_hazards gives correct sides/carries matching reality: hole 7 par-5 dogleg-right (polyline 346->36->55deg) bunkers R 170/430/520 + L 355/525; hole 11 par-4 bunkers both sides L 245/415 R 270/325/420. All normal/correct non-numbered guide phrasings PASS the validator. (b) ruled out on-hole — 7/11 have ONLY bunkers, nearest water 395-431y OFF the played line (not in play); a water/OB mention is therefore a CORRECT rejection (OSM has no 'ob' polygon type so any OB phrase always rejects — expected). (c) CONFIRMED + reproduced: _side_and_carry_supported() checks a claimed carry vs each bunker's CENTROID +-25y, but Bethpage bunkers are large — hole 7's right cross-bunker centroid=170y actually SPANS 103-280y (178y wide). A web-researched guide naturally citing any carry in the real footprint (220/240/250/260) but >25y from centroid -> WHOLE guide REJECTED (demonstrated: all four reject). That is why grounded, reasonable numbered advice keeps failing = the persistence pattern. DECISION NEEDED (genuine product/risk call, not an obvious fix): (c1) widen the carry check to the bunker's surveyed near->far carry SPAN (recovers guides on big-bunker holes) — but this loosens the grounding/anti-laundering control that had a bypass closed just days ago (5e4b861 carry-tie-break laundering), so it REQUIRES the full adversarial treatment: Fable plan + eval-harness teeth (each new accept-case proven RED pre-fix) + adversarial review proving no false number laundering re-opens; vs (c2) accept status quo — honest-empty is SAFE and correct (caddie falls back to the grounded generic hazard line); large-bunker holes simply get no cached prose guide. Recommend (c1) as a properly-reviewed future build item IF cached guides on such holes are wanted; otherwise close as working-as-designed. Optional confirmation: an on-box research pass capturing the exact rejected text would pin which number/phrase fired in prod, but the local reproduction already demonstrates the mechanism — not worth an unattended prod SSM action. FILES: backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py (_side_and_carry_supported, _has_side_flip, _CARRY_TOLERANCE_YARDS=25); backend/app/caddie/hazards.py (extract_hole_hazards, centroid-based carry). FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md (geometry/validation change without full adversarial treatment).
items.resolution: diagnosed — decision needed (do NOT inline-fix: validator is the anti-injection/grounding control)

items.title: Swap native JWT storage backend from UserDefaults to Keychain plugin
items.rationale: Final part of clerk-jwt-keychain. Replace @capacitor/preferences (UserDefaults, plaintext, backup-exposed) with @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage using kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so the Clerk JWT is encrypted at rest + excluded from device/iCloud backups. Thanks to native-token-store.ts this is a single-file backend swap (+ add the plugin + cap sync). NOTE: adds a native dependency (SPM/pod) and the actual Keychain persistence can only be fully verified on a real device/sim sign-in -> bundle with other device-verifiable changes or verify via a credentialed sim run before shipping. REQUIRED before App Store / wider release; NOT beta-blocking (owner-only TestFlight).
review_followups: LOW (TOCTOU, reviewer 2026-06-28): an in-flight FAPI response can re-persist the JWT just after clearNativeToken() on sign-out. Security-nil (Clerk destroys the session server-side; re-persisted token is revoked). Fix when doing the Keychain swap: gate setNativeToken on a module-level 'signed out' flag that clearNativeToken sets and a sign-in transition (false->true) resets — taking care NOT to suppress the re-sign-in token write (it happens during the sign_in FAPI exchange, before isSignedIn flips).

LOW (cold-start stale token, reviewer 2026-06-28): if sign-out happens while the app is killed, the stale (already server-invalid) JWT isn't cleared until it's injected and rejected. Fix: in nativeOnAfterResponse, clearNativeToken() on a FAPI authentication-failure error code — but first confirm the EXACT Clerk FAPI error code via a credentialed run so a valid token is never wrongly wiped.
items.defer_note: Auth/JWT storage swap + new native dependency (@aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage) + needs /security-review. Login breakage on real builds went uncaught before (see memory qa-test-critical-flows). Build in an ATTENDED session so login can be device-verified before ship — not unattended loop work.

items.title: USGS 3DEP COG ingestion pipeline (per course)
items.rationale: Offline job: fetch 3DEP 1m bare-earth DEM for a course bbox, build a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, upload to S3; read via rasterio/rio-tiler (windowed). Add rasterio/rio-tiler/boto3. Pilot on a handful of courses. First real heavy lift. Risks: CRS/data gaps/S3 cost.

items.title: Per-hole elevation profile precompute + store
items.rationale: Sample tee→green line + green grid from the COG into a hole_elevation_profiles table; replaces live USGS EPQS for profiled courses (EPQS stays fallback). Enables cheap plays-like lookups.

items.title: Target-point plays-like (any aim point, not just green center)
items.rationale: Cheap elevation lookup for an arbitrary aim/target lat-lng from the profile/COG so plays-like is correct for layups and the optimizer's chosen target. Cache windowed reads for latency.

Two activation paths: (1) on-demand — caddie.py start_session line ~324 seeds guides for a cold course on first play (RUNS in prod if prod ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + web_search work); (2) run_guide_backfill env-gated, EMPTY allowlist default → dormant unless GUIDE_BACKFILL_COURSES set. BLOCKING GAP (owner's 'test-critical-flows' memory): the live research path (web_search_20260209 + messages.parse + pause_turn continuation) was NEVER smoke-tested against a live key — and it's best-effort, so a failure is SILENT (guides never appear, no error). NEEDS: a bounded prod live-smoke (one hole/one course via SSM on-box, ~small API spend — OWNER OK since it spends prod budget) to confirm guides actually generate + are grounded; if broken, fix. Security/code review of the web-content-into-LLM surface (plan §9) also owed. | OWNER UNPAUSED 2026-07-09: 'Maybe a tiny bit of context added there would help to understand the shot and hole better.' Confirmed today the caddie has NO researched course knowledge (only our geometry/hazards/weather/player context). PHASE 1 (build): an LLM writer researches each hole (web search + our stored geometry as GROUND TRUTH — the guide may never contradict real hazards/yardage; HAZARD_GR
items.resolution: PROD LIVE-SMOKE VERIFIED 2026-07-11 (owner OK'd): research_hole_guide→validate_guide runs end-to-end vs prod key (claude-sonnet-5 + web_search + messages.parse + pause_turn); 37 guides already persisted (Bethpage Black 18 + Pebble 19); fresh Bethpage-7 smoke returned a real grounded guide (cites real bunkers/trees at correct dists/sides), validate_guide PASSED. items.title: Persist static course intel (elevation deltas + green slope) once per course
items.why: Owner ask (2026-07-08): elevation deltas + green slope are static per course. Compute once EVER per course, persist on the mapped course record (backend), serve instantly to course-intel. Kills the owner's 'elevation takes a while' wait. Backend-only (course record); no app-visible change beyond speed.
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-11 (bundle #133 merged @4bb474e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.0.1312 build 202607111242). Was: shipped-to-bundle (integration/next, PR #133). SILENT (backend-only; observable change = SPEED). Cycle 86 (2026-07-11). KEY FINDING on trace: ~90% was ALREADY BUILT in prior commits 0200576 + 9c5e338 — static per-hole intel (tee/green elevation, delta_ft, plays_like_yards, green_slope) is persisted in hole_features.properties JSONB, read instantly by build_hole_intelligence (ZERO USGS/3DEP on hit), bulk-precomputed (_precompute_course_elevations, 2 batched 3DEP calls), backfilled-on-demand, and honest (USGS-None persists nothing, no fabricated zeros). Weather stays DYNAMIC (correct). Fable plan v2 (specs/course-intel-static-persistence-plan.md) RULED against a new courses.static_intel column + Alembic migration: static intel is per-HOLE and already lives correctly per-feature; a course-level column would duplicate a 2nd source of truth (worse). NO migration. This cycle closed the two remaining gaps: (A) elevation precompute is now wired into create_mapped/put_mapped (was only /session/start → raced the first course-intel open on a fresh course), ordered BEFORE guides; (B) content-addressed invalidation — a new elevation_coords_key (tee+green centers @6dp) stamped into the JSONB by the precompute; the skip now requires an exact key match (was 'has tee_elevation_ft'), so a geometry re-map recomputes stale elevation. Also stamps elevation_computed_at (observability). Precompute + 2 helpers moved verbatim caddie.py → NEW app/services/course_elevation.py (import hygiene, no cycle). Request-path write-back stays KEYLESS (samples client coords it can't vouch for → treated as needs-revalidation). Read/hot path UNCHANGED. Commits: plan @d2d322d, feature @f682f85, head @efb32f4. Reviewer (fresh, adversarial): SHIP — static-only, invalidation deterministic (no lng/lat transposition, both sides from one _feature_center pipeline), write-back keyless, no read-path regression, no fabrication, no new concurrency hazard, tests faithfully encode spec (no weakened assertions), ruff clean. QA gates green on head: ruff clean, non-DB pytest 2060 passed/92 skipped, REAL docker Postgres (postgis:16-3.4) integration 92 passed incl mapped-course-db 10/10 (3 new precompute cases: idempotency-via-key, remap-invalidation round-trip, graceful-degrade), frontend lint/tsc clean + voice 277/277 (frontend untouched). Rides bundle #133 — no ship/ping (owner approves the whole bundle on next noticeable change).

Fully specced+gated; reopen ONLY if the coverage flywheel finds a JS-render-gated, non-anti-bot engine with no replayable XHR. | NOTE 2026-07-10: EZLinks/Cloudflare-Turnstile sites are NOT scrapable politely even with a headless browser (probe confirmed Chromium never clears Turnstile) — do NOT target them with S4d. S4d headless is only for JS-rendered widgets WITHOUT anti-bot gates. Re-scope S4d's target engine after S4a/S4c reveal which engines actually need a browser. The only slice with deploy-image impact (~400MB Chromium, gated TEETIME_SCRAPER_ENABLED default off). In-process asyncio worker + bounded queue off the request path; TeeTimeSlot gains status live|pending; poll endpoint; frontend pending row; first browser-scraped engine (Club Prophet or hostile EZLinks). Plan §4b,§6,§7. Depends on S4a.

items.title: Harden guide_writer pause_turn continuation re-serialization (low-pri)
items.why: Prod smoke (2026-07-11) noted: guide_writer.py:278 re-serializes assistant content via model_dump(mode='json') in the pause_turn continuation loop; when the model returns code_execution/text_editor_code_execution server-tool blocks (not web_search), pydantic emits PydanticSerializationUnexpectedValue warnings. NON-FATAL (the smoke hole finished first-turn), but a hole needing a real pause_turn continuation with those block types may not re-serialize cleanly. Fix: resume with the SDK block objects directly rather than a manual model_dump. Low priority.

ui_decision_social_course: OWNER OVERRIDE (2026-06-28, IMG_2960): build a floating 'island' pill bottom tab bar (Instagram-style — rounded, hovers above the bottom edge, NOT edge-to-edge SaaS) for navigating the future features. This supersedes the earlier 'no bottom tab' recommendation. Build it in the yardage-book aesthetic (on-paper, restrained, calm — not generic SaaS) and HIDE it on immersive screens (active round / voice / GPS) where the app should disappear. Likely tabs: Round/Home · Partners · Courses (+ profile). This is the 'minimal home dock' path from the plan, now promoted to the chosen approach + extended to a persistent floating tab. A new card 'nav-floating-island-tab' tracks the build.
